name: node-is-extendable
title: node-is-extendable
summary: determine if a value can be extended
description:
determine if a value can be extended
Returns true if a value is any of the object types: array, regexp, plain
object, function or date. This is useful for determining if a value can be
extended, e.g. "can the value have keys?"
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-is-extendable because determine if a value can be extended.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-is-extendable as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-is-extendable with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-is-extendable --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
